sections.title: Speaking to Larry at Ardougne Zoo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Do you need any help? I'm looking for a quest.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Are you working for them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, I'm not sure. Who are they?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: EXACTLY!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seriously, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I think you can be trusted. What do you know about...

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: penguins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...they live in the cold? That's about all.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Would you like to learn more about them? I'm conducting a, uh, research project, to, uh, learn about their social habits in their natural environment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need my help for?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, I need to build a shelter to hide from view, and it's always useful to have an extra pair of eyes, you know, for, uh, observing...Penguins.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: So, are you up for it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, why not! But first, who are you and why are you researching penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I, uh, was a zoo keeper here. But, I, uh, am on leave to go research penguins in their native habitat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok! Okay then. So, what did you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Right. I need you to gather materials for the bird hide, while I arrange for our transport.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You need to get 10 oak planks, 10 steel nails, a hammer, and a spade so that we can build a bird hide.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Once you have those, meet me up by the entrance to Keldagrim, east of Rellekka. I'll be there with a boat to take us to our destination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, oak planks, steel nails, hammer, spade, east of Rellekka. Got it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

sections.title: Getting materials to build the hideout

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Have you got the equipment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have them with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to get?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You have all the materials. Off to the iceberg!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Once you have the items, meet me up by the entrance to Keldagrim, east of Rellekka. I'll be there with a boat to take us to our destination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no idea where that is.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: And you call yourself an adventurer! There is a road from Catherby going north. Take it until you reach the Sinclair Mansion.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Take a left at the Mansion, cross two bridges and follow the road until you reach Rellekka. Head east, around Rellekka to the coast near the entrance of Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Did I spell that out enough for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No need to be snippy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I know where to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how do I make a bird hide?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Use the planks on the patch of snow on that ridge. Once you have that constructed, use the spade on the snow to cover the hide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I've made the structure. Now what?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Not bad... Now you need to cover the bird hide in snow with a spade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we start observing the penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Yes. Let's get in and start observing. You should note down anything interesting the penguins do. I'll include it in my, uh, research.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Looks like the penguins are on patrol again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're just walking around, Larry.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: They are organised! ORGANISED I tell you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we start observing the penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Yes. Let's get in and start observing. You should note down anything interesting the penguins do. I'll include it in my, uh, research.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Look, here come two penguins now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww, they're cute.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: EVIL. I mean, how fascinating.

sections.title: Speaking to Larry again without leaving

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: We must get away from here before we discuss our plans. We don't want to be overheard.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I knew it. I knew they were up to something! Did you see that? They were patrolling!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: It isn't! They saluted and recognised each other. They're organised!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looked like one of them shivered and the other one just walked past. Nothing suspicious there, it is really cold here!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I don't care what you think. I know they are doing something. No one ever believes me. But I'll prove them wrong. ALL OF THEM.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, calm down. What do you think we should do now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It did seem rather suspicious.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You believe me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I mean, those penguins were acting quite strangely.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: No one ever believes me. Can I...hug you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whoa there, I don't think this calls for hugging. What do we do next about the penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: We need to get closer to the penguins. Infiltrate them. Learn what they are doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you plan to do that?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: We can't discuss it here; there are too many spies. Let's leave the iceberg and then I'll tell you my plans.

sections.title: Speaking to Larry at the Zoo or Relekka

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you plan to infiltrate the penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I have altered some designs of clockwork toys to make a clockwork penguin suit. We will use this to infiltrate their ranks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? We'll just set it off walking? What good will that do?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: No! I'm going to use a spell to shrink you down so you can control it from the inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute, why am I being shrunk? Why can't you get in the suit and annoy the penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: The penguins know my scent. They'd find me out and capture me and...torture me. No, it has to be you.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Don't worry. The spell only works if I'm nearby since I have to be constantly casting it. If trouble starts I'll be close by to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gee, that's comforting.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Here's the book. It has my designs and a list of things you need to get. Use your Crafting table at your house to build it, just like regular clockwork toys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I have to make the suit? I'm tired of doing all the legwork.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You have to make it, because since I got fired from the Zoo I've been homeless. Thanks for caring.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry... Hey, you told me you were on leave to do research!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well...it's permanent leave, and I am doing research! When I used to work at the Zoo, I noticed the penguins doing odd things, like taking notes or timing things. I asked to have my work detail transferred to their cage and I was fired!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I'm sure the penguins are behind it. So I kept spying on them and I'll prove everyone wrong!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's why you can't wear the suit? Because you used to work near them?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Err, no. I've had a few run-ins since then. But I don't have time for war stories, we need to concentrate on our mission!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: When you're done making the suit, we'll head back to the iceberg and try it out.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Do you have the suit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You don't seem to have the suit with you.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: That's perfect. You are a very skilled crafter. Now we must return to the iceberg and test it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the clockwork book, could I have another?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You haven't lost it, you just need to look for it more closely.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, lucky for you I did make another copy. But be more careful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a bit confused. Can you explain how to make the suit?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You need 1 piece of silk, 1 clockwork mechanism, and 1 wood plank.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: The book will explain how to make the suit. But, since you seem to lazy to read it, I'll summarise.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Take the materials to your house and use the Crafting table to make clockwork toys. From there you can make the suit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not done yet.

sections.title: Speaking to Larry again without leaving

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I can teleport us back to the Zoo, but you'll have to make your own way back, teleports tire me out.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Look what they did to the bird hide! It's completely destroyed! The savages. The penguins are on to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it was the penguins. You can't even make out the prints clearly, it could have been anything.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well of course you can't make out tracks. Penguins walk single file to hide their numbers! They're clever...so very clever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks like a warning message to keep us away. I didn't think penguins were violent by nature.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, they act like they aren't, but it's all a guise. They wanted to scare us off. We must be on to something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there aren't any penguins around now, so we can't try out the suit.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: But, we were so close to finding out what exactly is going on. It's all ruined now. How will we ever infiltrate them if there are none around to infiltrate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are other penguins. What about the ones at the Zoo? At least we can test the suit on them.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: OF COURSE! You are brilliant. I should have thought of that. It's where all of this started. Let's return to the Zoo.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I can teleport us back to the Zoo. It'll be faster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why the whole malarkey with the boat?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Boat rides lower my stress level.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I'm too tired to teleport again. You'll have to get back to the Zoo on your own.

sections.title: Testing the suit at the zoo
Speaking to Larry at Ardougne Zoo
Speaking to Larry while in the suit

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Don't talk to me while you're in the suit! It'll give away your cover. Here, I'll get you out of it.

sections.title: Using Larry's Tuxedo-time while in the suit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm ready to be big again! Please get me out of this.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: All right, keep calm. One growth spurt coming up.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: After I put you in the suit, use the little gate in the enclosure to get inside. The zoo keepers won't notice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guess we should try this out. How do you know a shrinking spell, anyway?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, a while back I was out in the field observing penguins. I worked for the Ardougne Zoo then. I was just quietly observing them when suddenly there was an avalanche.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Larry, you are one unlucky dude.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I'm not done! I was trapped, but a passing old man stopped to help. He said he would shrink me so I could crawl out from under the weight of the snow.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Then he taught me the spell in case I would have need of it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like a pretty wise old man.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You see, that incident made me start suspecting the penguins, and my further work with them at the Zoo just confirmed it. I'm sure they got me fired. I know too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well we don't have all day. Time to be a penguin!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Okay, but remember, if you move too far away from me the spell will break and you'll be normal sized again. Also, if you need to switch back, just ask me and I'll unshrink you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, right. Let's just get this over with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Penguin time!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You will need your hands free to operate the suit.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You can't wear a cape inside the suit, it might get caught.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Squack! Quack, quackity squack, squee.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: What do you think you are doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm, uh, speaking penguin, I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WAIT. How come you can speak in the common tongue? You're a penguin!

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: ...So are you.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: And you are speaking in the common tongue. So, what is your point?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right. Yes, of course! Uh, I was just, uh, testing you. Making sure you're on your toes, claws, whatever.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Operatives must not speak without giving the proper greeting. You have not given the proper greeting and until you do I have nothing more to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I better try and get this greeting right.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: I am sorry comrade, but you still have not shown me the proper greeting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Whew, that was right. Two more moves to do. If I get those right, he should speak to me.*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *I just need to do one more move and I'll have gotten the greeting right.*

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Welcome comrade. Now we may speak properly.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: What news do you bring? Have you made progress with the main directive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, the main directive? Yeah...sure, I made progress with it. It was...easy?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: It was easy!? It was easy to learn how to FLY?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, yeah. I just kind of jumped off a hill and flapped a lot.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: You flapped a lot? I didn't think we could fly like other birds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I also launched myself out of a cannon!

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: This caused you to fly... ingenious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I flew in a hot air balloon.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: A hot air balloon? This contraption made you fly?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: This is very amazing. I will make note of it in my mission report.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is this so important? I mean, we're good swimmers, who cares if we can't fly?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Who cares? It's the entire reason we are in this wretched cage! We need to learn the secrets of flight, and the best way is to get these gullible humans to reveal it to us unwittingly. Blast them and their thumbs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound kind of bitter.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Yes, I am. I'm tired of observing these humans and being gawked at by nasty little children. I miss the fresh codfish of our motherland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: You could take my mission report back to the outpost for me. I don't want to risk breaking out and blowing our cover.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: You should check in with the operatives in Lumbridge before you head to the outpost. They might have some news for you to take as well.

sections.title: After speaking with Ardougne Zoo's penguins

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The penguin gave me his mission report and told me to go to Lumbridge to speak to the penguins there and then go to 'the outpost'.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Lumbridge? There aren't any penguins in Lumbridge. Not that I've seen. Where is this 'outpost'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the penguins in Lumbridge are undercover?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't find out where their outpost is. I'm hoping the ones in Lumbridge will tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems kind of crazy that these penguins have mission reports and operatives.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: They're organised little devils. Don't underestimate them.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You go on ahead to Lumbridge. I'll meet you there and put you in the suit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They don't even have thumbs. How organised can they be?

sections.title: Searching for the penguins at Lumbridge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, we're in Lumbridge. Where are these penguins?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I was chatting with a local farmer, and there is a rumour about a 'thing' that lives around here. Maybe it's the penguin and these simple people don't recognise them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably do the secret handshake before I try talking to these penguins.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: What do you think you are doing? You can't even get the greeting right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Whew, that was right. Two more moves to do. If I get those right, he should speak to me.*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *I just need to do one more move and I'll have gotten the greeting right.*

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: What do you think you are doing? You're going to blow our cover. Get away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I gave the correct greeting. You have to talk to me.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: Every two-bit soldier knows that salute. We won't give you the time of the day without the secret phrase. Now GO AWAY.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the penguins at the Zoo sent me. They spoke to me without some phrase.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: They are lazy, good for nothing codfish addicts. GO AWAY.

sections.title: Returning to Larry about a secret phrase

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The penguins won't talk to me without some stupid secret phrase. These penguins are crazy!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: No, I keep trying to tell you, they aren't crazy, they are dangerous and organised.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, since the penguin at the Zoo is the only one talking to you, maybe you should ask him.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I can teleport us back to the Zoo, but you'll have to make your own way back. Teleports tire me out.

sections.title: Asking the Zoo penguin the phrase

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The penguins in Lumbridge refuse to talk to me. They keep demanding a secret phrase.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: So, tell them the phrase. It's not exactly hard to remember. Pescaling Pax has a strange sense of humour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pesca-what?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Pescaling Pax. The Pescallion's son. Well, one of them. He's in charge of research and development at our headquarters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, where all of the penguins...I mean us...live?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Comrade! You really do have a funny sense of humour. No, our people live in the great city of Palingrad, of course. Ah, home, I miss it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound pretty homesick. Can't you go and visit Palingrad?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: If only. The Pescallion has declared that until we have finished our research of the southern species and have developed our great plan, we may not return. So I am stuck here observing humans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gee, I sure would like to get back, too. If only you'd tell me the phrase, so I could get the other mission report and move on.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: How do you not know the phrase, comrade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, I forgot what the phrase was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was very careless of you, comrade. But it is your problem, not mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must have left the outpost before they gave out the phrase.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: No one left before it was given out. I was one of the first operatives to leave, and I know it.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: What? Comrade, I think you need your head examined when you get back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need that phrase! Isn't there anything you can do?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: As you are a comrade, I suppose it is my duty to help you.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: The phrase is 'Do not trust the walrus'.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: I see you have some raw cod with you comrade. I might be willing to exchange information for it, if you are willing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Thank you comrade. This is like a taste of the motherland. The phrase is 'Do not trust the walrus.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, no I'll hang on to it.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: I am sorry comrade, but I cannot break protocol without good reason.

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Haa haa haa, that is a funny joke. I like your sense of humour comrade. It would be like asking 'Where is the motherland'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean they aren't the same?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Haa haa haa... oh you have me in stitches.

sections.title: Not getting the phrase from the Zoo penguin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Zoo penguin won't tell me the phrase.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Maybe there's something he wants, something you can trade for the phrase? Or maybe there's some kind of magic that would persuade him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, what do penguins like?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I once had to clean all the penguins feet, back when I had a job.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I distracted them by giving them cod fish and then did the job. It was gross.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I ever want to be a Zoo keeper.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Or perhaps there's some sort of magic that could persuade the penguin? An object of power or the like?

lines.speaker: Penguin
lines.text: Yes, comrade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need that phrase! Isn't there anything you can do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got the phrase! These penguins are so weird. It's something about a walrus.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You aren't taking this seriously! They are dangerous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, and organised. I know.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, just go talk to the sheep.

sections.title: Returning to the "sheep" with the phrase

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do not trust the walrus.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: Fine. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The penguin at the Zoo told me to see if you had any news.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: We can't get any news in this field! All these humans keep trying to shear us!

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: We think the Farmer may be an agent for the other side, trying to have us assassinated by all these foolish humans. If you investigate him for us, we can finish our mission report and you can go on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to disguise myself as a human and talk to the Farmer? Seems like a tough request. I'll only do it if you tell me how to get to the outpost.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: What do you mean 'How to get to the outpost'? Any intelligent penguin knows that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you calling me dumb? Fine. You can talk to the Farmer yourself, and compromise your mission.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: No, no, we'll tell you. You just deal with the Farmer.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about penguins.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: About what now? What do penguins have to do with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Fred! You've been messing with the wrong people. You better leave the penguins alone... OR ELSE!

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you shouting about? Don't you come into my house shouting at me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean it Fred, I don't know whose side you're working for, but assassinating the penguin operatives is going to get you in trouble. To think you'd even stoop to sending others to try and 'shear' them. Be careful, or you might find you've been shorn.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Is this some kind of joke? I'm a SHEEP farmer. That means I need to shear sheep. What are these penguins you keep talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't play neutral with me, Fred. I see right through you. You're probably working for the squirrels, aren't you? I'll be watching Fred. I'm always watching.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know that 'Thing' out in your field? It's two penguins in disguise.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Like I haven't heard that before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No really, it is! They're trying to observe humans to learn our weaknesses.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Then why are they disguised as sheep?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They probably thought they'd blend in better. Anyway, I came to warn you to not annoy them too much. They're funny little guys but they seem to have a grudge against you.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: You talk to these penguins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have a penguin suit and I know the secret greeting. I'm infiltrating them because this crazy guy Larry is convinced they are trying to take over the world or something.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Look, when these voices in your head start talking to you again, you might want to ignore them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: HEY! I'm not crazy! Larry's a little left of centre, but I'm just helping him out. The penguins really can talk.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Right, right. I believe you, sure. Maybe on your way out you could ask the sheep to herd themselves for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. Don't believe me. Larry and I will prove you wrong. Just don't go near the penguins, Fred, not if you value your life.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: Is the Farmer an agent for the other side?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Farmer is definitely an enemy. High ranking too, I think. He might even be the King of Lumbridge in disguise!

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: King? I thought there was a duke, not a king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's why he's in disguise. Duh.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: This is an amazing revelation. We must include it in our report. Thank you, we'll mention your bravery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Farmer is harmless. The sheep, however...now they seem pretty crafty.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: The sheep? But...They just wander around in this field all day. Trust us, we know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly! So you don't suspect them! That's why they are so cunning. Watch your backs.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: You have a good point. Yes, we must warn Pescaling Pax of this new threat. We'll put it in the report and you can give it to them.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: Well, off you go now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey wait, you said you'd tell me where the outpost is!

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: You are one clueless penguin. The outpost is the iceberg to the North. The entrance is concealed by what looks like an avalanche.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: When you try and go through a guard will stop you and ask for the password.

lines.speaker: Sheep
lines.text: The password is cabbage. Don't forget it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Haa haa, this is fun!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: What? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just convinced them Fred the Farmer was the King of Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: But there is no King of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly! They are so gullible. Even if they are organised.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Don't take this so lightly. You might put Fred in danger because you were having a laugh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. It was just a bit of fun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just convinced the penguins that the sheep are agents for the other side.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: They are?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! They're just sheep. But I wanted to confuse the penguins. Put them off our scent.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: What a good plan! I just hope the sheep aren't working for the other side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Larry, we need to get you to hang out with people, not animals.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know where their outpost is! The entrance is on the iceberg. It's concealed by an avalanche.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Great. Let's go back to the iceberg, I'll put you in your suit and we'll really see what these penguins are up to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Now you can try and enter the outpost. This is exciting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's fun for you! You're not risking your neck.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: But you don't think penguins are dangerous anyway. So there's no danger is there? Or are you coming around to my thinking that they are dangerous?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No...I mean...yes. Oh never mind. Just do the spell. Wait, won't the spell wear off once I'm inside? You'll be too far away.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, no. The ice magnifies this spell, so it will allow you to go greater distances.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This guy looks official. I'd better do the greeting before I speak to him.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Don't stand around, agent. You look absurd.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Whew, that was right. Two more moves to do. If I get those right, he should speak to me.*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *I just need to do one more move and I'll have gotten the greeting right.*

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Password?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cabbage.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Let's see your ID and you can go through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ID? No one told me anything about ID.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: No ID, no entry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The penguin at the entrance said I needed to have an ID. Nobody told me about any ID!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: ID? But...they all look alike. Well, the other penguins probably just assumed you had it, since you said you were an agent.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You know, I met a rather shifty looking penguin once, north of Miscellania. He was named after some sort of pasta...

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Anyway, he worked for a black market. Maybe he has a contact here who could get you an ID?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Penguins have black markets?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Of course. Just look around here for a shifty pasta penguin.

sections.title: Obtaining an ID from the black market

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Oi, mate, 'u innerested i' sum 'wares?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...maybe?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: I go' me sum hot 'wares, mate. Wotcha need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've misplaced me, I mean my, ID card.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Ah, frisky nigh' on leave, eh? Betcha furgo' yur missin repor' 'oo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, my missin repor'. I think I need one of them too.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Well, mate. 'U scratch me back, I stab urs, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No, no stabbing.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: I men' in a good way. I wan' sumpin in exy-change. Ima in need of sum swamp tur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tur? Do you mean tar?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Thas wha' I sed, innit? I need sum feathers 'oo. Foive of 'em.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you need swamp tar and five feathers. Will chicken feathers do?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Yea, begreat. Then I'll guv yea 'ur stuff.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Need summat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I need some replacements.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: U dunna need ID.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Oi, 'u got 'ur repor'.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Yuv go' the stuff?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I got it.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Whers me swamp tur 'n feathers, mate?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Whers me feathers, mate?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Whers me swamp tur, mate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you need?

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: I need swamp tur and foive feathers, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this black market you run. Seems kind of risky.

lines.speaker: Noodle
lines.text: Nah ain't risky. Bu' I dun run it. Me bruva, Alfredo, does. E runs the Fettuccine Mafia.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiight. Fettucine Mafia. I'll remember that.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Let's see your ID and you can go through.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: No ID, no entry!

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: All right, you can go in. The entrance is hidden under that avalanche.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Once inside, go into the room to the left. You'll be debriefed there. No dawdling!

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Welcome back agent...

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Peach? But, aren't you...male?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was either that or Pear.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Ah. Of course.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Pablo? But, aren't you...female?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hippie parents, sir. They had weird ideas about names.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: So you have just returned from your mission?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and I have my mission report. I also have the mission reports from the operatives in the Ardougne Zoo and the Lumbridge sheep field.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: How very industrious of you, agent. Please hand them over.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Hmm, these reports are...intriguing.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: It says you jumped off a hill and flew? Without assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah. It was amazing. You know, I just...flapped. Really fast.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Hmm, we shall have to research this.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Did you really get launched out of a cannon? It seems quite risky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't too bad. I was wearing a helmet at the time.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: This could be very useful during battle.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: You flew in a 'hot air balloon'? What is that exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it was a sack of cloth with a basket attached to the bottom and when you filled it with hot air it went up.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Fascinating. Well done agent.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: I am satisfied with your reports. You just need to complete a physical and you will be free to roam at your leasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A physical? What, are they going to examine my body?

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Oh no. It's an agility course to make sure you're still fit and able to serve. Don't worry, I'm sure you won't have any difficulty getting through it. Just go through the door at the end of the room.

sections.title: Speaking with the army commander outside the course

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All those penguins, what are they doing?

lines.speaker: Army Commander
lines.text: Ah, they are doing drills! Getting ready for the invasion. What a glorious day that will be!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Invasion? Of...

lines.speaker: Army Commander
lines.text: Yes, yes, I know we all get excited. But, I need you to get out of the way. Move along!

lines.speaker: Agility Instructor
lines.text: Well done soldier! You've successfully completed the physical. You're free to do as you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do now?

lines.speaker: Agility Instructor
lines.text: Just go through the gate in the fence and use the same door you came out of. You can come back whenever you like to tone up your body.

sections.title: Reporting to Larry after finding about penguin training

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Larry, you were right! There's an entire army of them, and they're drilling and... Larry, they're ORGANISED!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: That's what I've been saying from the start! But an entire army? This is just an outpost, so it can't be an entire army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what I saw! They were doing drills and everything.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Still, it must only be an invasion party. The main force is on...well, it doesn't matter right now.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I've been doing some research of my own, and I think I know what our next step should be. There's a secret room, deep in the glacier. I think that is where they are making their war plans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: War plans! Who are they going to war with?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: With us, you dolt! They are trying to take over the world. Have you not grasped this yet? You need to find that room. Our future depends on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where could this room be?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: It's probably the most well guarded room in the place. You'll need to find a way to get into it without arousing suspicion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a pretty tall order.

sections.title: Speaking to Larry again before looking for the war room

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: What are you doing out here! You need to get in there and find the secret room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keep your shorts on; I'm working on it.

sections.title: Talking to the agent near the large door

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You there, let me through those doors.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Let's see your ID, comrade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem!

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: I'm sorry comrade, you do not have the clearance to go through these doors.

sections.title: Asking penguins in a room nearby about the door
Asking Larry about the cowbell and bongos

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have a lead. These two penguin bards seem to know a lot of the history of the iceberg. I think they might know a way into the War Room.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Bards? They have bards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, that's what I said.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I get a cowbell or a set of bongos?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I'm not even going to ask why.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I think dairy cows have bells, you could steal one from it. Bongos... I suppose you could make them? Mahogany is good for bending, and then just use soft leather for the drum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good plan, Larry.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Man, did you bring the instruments to us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I need to get you?

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: We want a cowbell and a set of bongos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: You didn't bring the instruments.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: You didn't bring the bongos.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: You didn't bring a cowbell.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Dude, good job on the instruments. Just let us set up and we'll get going.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: And a 1 and a 2 and a 3...

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Wait, wait. It needs more cowbell.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: You're right, man. Let's try this again!

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: There once was a Pescallion, who travelled in a galleon.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Our Pescallion went exploring and found this iceberg

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: He travelled the world, until his toes curled.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Upon an iceberg he came, and our lives were never the same.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: As an outpost it was approved, so here our people moved.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: To grow and learn, though our hearts did yearn...

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: For the motherland.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Deep inside the 'berg lay a threat, but don't fret!

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: For Pescaling Pax trapped those monsters behind a gate, but let's hope they don't mate.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Now we have no fear

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: But shed a small tear for the motherland.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: All right, that's enough racket.

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: Shut those instruments up or I'm throwing you in solitary!

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: I don't care if your vibes are harmonious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now might be a good time for me to slip out and get through those doors...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look like...knowledgeable...penguins. What's through those doors at the end of the hall?

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Hey man, don't stress. Those doors open new worlds, man. Like, new rooms too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, well, I would like to know what rooms.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Like, the War Room and stuff like that, dude. Wicked stuff. Too bad only 'the man' gets to go through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Humans can go through?

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: No, man. He just means the authority. The MAN, you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? You don't really look like KGP agents.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Dude, we are bards. We sing of the glories of the common penguin. We are also supposed to record history, but man, it is so HIS story. Like, the MAN's story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Penguins have bards?

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: Totally, dude. You want to hear one of our songs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I haven't got anything to lose, except my eardrums.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Well, like, we can't play it in THIS now. Maybe at a later now. We don't have any instruments to accompany our great song.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But this room is filled with instruments.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Yeah man, but they come from the south, where humans have thumbs to play them with. We don't have thumbs. Also, the Pescaling had our old ones confiscated for being too loud.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Way harsh, man.

lines.speaker: Ping
lines.text: But dude, I was reading, and there are instruments we could play. I read about one called bongos. They just need to be hit.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: But we need two instruments, man. Like, to make it harmonious with the vibes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about a cowbell? You don't need thumbs for that.

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Righteous man. So you'll get a cowbell and bongos for us and we'll rock out. We'll be so harmonious, no Pescallion could complain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, why am I getting these instruments?

lines.speaker: Pong
lines.text: Because your aura says you should.

lines.speaker: Pescaling Pax
lines.text: And we'll move this little penguin here...and KABLOOM! Heee hee.

lines.speaker: Pescaling Pax
lines.text: It appears we have a visitor!

lines.speaker: Pescaling Pax
lines.text: Haa haa! I am glad we installed that anti-magic security. What have we here?

lines.speaker: Pescaling Pax
lines.text: What an ingenious little device. This will solve all our problems. Scientists! Come examine this; we have much work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about me?

lines.speaker: Pescaling Pax
lines.text: Hmm, take that human to the Ice Lords. They'll enjoy the exercise.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You're safe! I was really worried for a while there. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ice Lord...hit me...ow...

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Yes, yes, you're very brave. I mean what happened in the War Room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pescaling Pax was there, and he forced me out of the suit and then threw me into a cage with the Ice Lords!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: But what was IN the War Room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There was a map of the world and a lot of little penguin figures.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: YES! I knew it! I said I'd prove them all wrong. They are organised and invading. OH YEAH, Larry was right. Who was right? Larry was!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: LARRY! We've just uncovered a plot that could destroy everything we hold dear. Don't you think we should focus on stopping them?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You are such a buzz kill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to plan our next move, but now they know both our scents and might come after us!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: No, they only know mine. That agility course really stank up the suit and seems to have covered your scent. You might want to consider bathing soon.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: As for our next move, I've got...friends...elsewhere who should hear of this information, but we have plenty of time. It's not as though they made a break through in their research or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The hide was destroyed. It's too damaged to rebuild, so we can't use it to observe penguins any more.

sections.title: Trying to manually unequip the penguin suit
Attempting to speak to "sheep" as a human

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't speak to the penguins when I'm not in my disguise.

sections.title: Trying to use the shortcut near Fred as a penguin
When caught as a human at the iceberg

lines.speaker: KGP Agent
lines.text: There's been a breach! Red alert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urrrrrgh!

lines.speaker: Dairy cow
lines.text: MOO

sections.title: Trying to leave the Ice lord cage before defeating them
